mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present values and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals an imminent economic slowdown, triggering risk-off positioning that depresses S&P 500 prices over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 50.0 thousand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and consumer credit growth indicates a synchronized withdrawal of household spending, which compresses corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST decline AND RSAFS decline) fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05), or single-series models outperform the joint signal in out-of-sample Sharpe ratios.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects pent-up demand absorption and capacity utilization normalization, which boosts corporate earnings visibility and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative or flat 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of observations during the specified regime, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for earnings revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the aggregate earnings base, which mechanically supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter acceleration consistently fails to correlate with positive 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or forward returns are indistinguishable from random walk benchmarks.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, the cost of capital becomes structurally elevated, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.01 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth deceleration and unemployment rate elevation signal a deteriorating labor market equilibrium, which triggers recession fears and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition (PAYEMS decline AND UNRATE rise) does not produce a statistically significant negative return differential compared to baseline (p > 0.05), or the interaction effect is fully absorbed by single-factor models.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion with cooling demand, rising inflation combined with stagnant GDP growth triggers stagflation repricing, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases alongside flat/negative GDPC1 growth fail to predict negative 5-day forward returns in > 50% of observations, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in out-of-sample testing.
